title: Static Delivery, HTTP Representations, URLs, Bases, and Origins · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for static delivery, http representations, urls, bases, and origins and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, filesystem, parser, template, validator, host, CDN, DNS, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for versioned URL and delivery contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Trace a browser request through URL resolution, origin, path mapping, representation selection, cache, and response metadata.
Keep platform filesystem paths, POSIX publication paths, percent-encoded URL paths, queries, and fragments as distinct types.
Model root, project-subpath, preview, custom-domain, trailing-slash, and relative-link behavior under one explicit base URL.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz publishing team handles case 5565 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile static requests, base-path URL resolution, routes, fragments, and representation metadata; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. stimulus.title: Http Evidence failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: required

options.text: Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.
Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority. That claim does not survive the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary.

For diagnose the hidden failure, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Http Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Http Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz publishing team handles case 7807 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile static requests, base-path URL resolution, routes, fragments, and representation metadata; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Verify media type, character encoding, caching, compression, conditional delivery, range needs, and error behavior on the target host.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Http Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Http Evidence, keep Http Evidence connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify media type, character encoding, caching, compression, conditional delivery, range needs, and error behavior on the target host.
